descriptionInUI: Runtime for the 'Distant ground AI sleeps' performance setting. Inert unless the mission generator emits dcsRetribution.aiSleep (the setting is on and an eligible group exists). Each emitted group has its AI switched off at mission start -- it keeps existing (visible, strikeable, kills record normally), it just stops running sensors and targeting -- and is woken whenever any aircraft, either side's, closes inside the wake radius, then put back to sleep once the sky above it is clear. Eligibility is decided entirely by the generator, never by this plugin: rear-area garrisons always, plus short-range AAA gun sites when 'Also sleep short-range AAA gun sites' is on. The front line, convoys, missile sites and every scripted mover are excluded there. The wake radius is floored at 10 NM, which is what makes the sleep invisible: a garrison's embedded short-range escorts, and every gun site the generator lets through, see far less than that, so they are always thinking again well before anything enters their envelope. Raising the radius is always safe; it only costs performance.

specificOptions.nameInUI: Wake radius: aircraft within this range wake a group (NM)
Startup grace before the first sleep pass (s)
